propositions: Measure how a candidate change to the plan-review gate rubric (R1) perturbs verdicts of plans that already passed and closed cleanly, before exposing it to production.
Control recall contamination by running each fresh gate over a cloned ticket store with all REVIEW_RESULT sidecar files deleted so prior-review recall is a no-op.

Control baseline drift and LLM nondeterminism by re-running both the current/main and candidate gates fresh over the same recall-stripped clones rather than comparing against the persisted sidecar baseline.
Coarse tier replays ~200 stratified cleanly-closed tickets once to produce a plan-v3-segmented transition matrix and a PASS-to-BLOCK flip rate held to a <=0.02 bar.

Precise tier over-samples a 50-ticket FP-control pool, runs 3 replays each of candidate and current, drops tickets with any INDETERMINATE verdict or material-fingerprint mismatch, and scores the first 30 fully-usable tickets via two-level median.
R1 fails only if candidate new-blocking median exceeds max(3, current-vs-current null floor) or new-advisory median delta exceeds 1, with metrics matched on a criteria-free tkey to avoid inflation.
